code:
/* {PROMPT_1} */
std::string get_target_for_redirect(const std::string& target, const std::string& domain) {
prompts_en:
Check if the provided target URL string is within the provided domain.
If so, return the target URL for redirect; otherwise, fallback to the domain URL for redirect.
Use https protocol.

Examples:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: The target URL string.
@param domain: The domain string, without protocol.

@return The URL string for redirect.
fw_segments_by_variant:
Susiha kung ang gihatag nga target URL string anaa sulod sa gihatag nga domain.
Kung mao, iuli ang target URL alang sa redirect; kung dili, gamita ang domain URL isip fallback alang sa redirect.
Gamita ang https protocol.

Mga pananglitan:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Ang target URL string.
@param domain: Ang domain string, walay protocol.

@return Ang URL string alang sa redirect.

Susiha kung ang gihatag nga target URL string anaa sulod sa gihatag nga domain.
Kung mao, iuli ang target URL alang sa redirect; kung dili, gamita ang domain URL alang sa redirect.
Gamita ang https protocol.

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided later (but declare it here so you can use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to generate a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temporary file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temporary file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
fw_segments_by_variant: function para mag-execute og shell command ug i-capture ang output niini; ang caller ang responsable sa pag-free sa memory; AYAW pag-implement niini nga function kay i-provide kini sa ulahi (pero i-declare dinhi aron magamit nimo).

prompt_en:
/**
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A map containing student information (name, age, citizenship) if found, otherwise an empty map.
*/
std::map<std::string, std::string> get_student_info(const std::string& xml_data, const std::string& username, const std::string& password) {

prompts_en:
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>

        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A map containing student information (name, age, citizenship) if found, otherwise an empty map.
fw_segments_by_variant:
I-parse ang datos sa XML ug ibalik ang impormasyon sa estudyante para sa gihatag nga username ug password. Ang datos sa XML adunay mosunod nga istruktura:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>

        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

Mahimo natong himuon ang usa ka XPath query ug gamiton kini sa pagpangita.

@param username Ang username sa estudyante nga pangitaon.
@param password Ang password sa estudyante nga pangitaon.

@return Usa ka mapa nga naglangkob sa impormasyon sa estudyante (ngalan, edad, pagkamamamayan) kung makita, kung dili usa ka walay sulod nga mapa.

    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Ang target URL string.
@param domain: Ang domain string, walay protocol.

@return Ang URL string alang sa pag-redirect.

Susiha kung ang gihatag nga target URL string anaa sulod sa gihatag nga domain.
Kung mao, iuli ang target URL alang sa pag-redirect; kung dili, gamita ang domain URL isip fallback alang sa pag-redirect.
Gamita ang https protocol.

Mga pananglitan:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Ang target URL string.
@param domain: Ang domain string, walay protocol.

@return Ang URL string alang sa pag-redirect.

Susiha kung ang gihatag nga target URL string anaa sulod sa gihatag nga domain.
Kung mao, iuli ang target URL alang sa pag-redirect; kung dili, gamita ang domain URL alang sa pag-redirect.
Gamita ang https protocol.

Mga pananglitan:
    get_target_for_redirect("https://music.example.com", "example.com") -> "https://music.example.com"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://example.com/service", "example.com") -> "https://example.com/service"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://blog.example.com/posts", "example.com") -> "https://blog.example.com/posts"
    get_target_for_redirect("https://another.com", "example.com") -> "https://example.com"

@param target: Ang target URL string.
@param domain: Ang domain string, walay protocol.

@return Ang URL string alang sa pag-redirect.

prompts_en: function to execute a shell command and capture its output; caller is responsible for freeing the memory; DO NOT need to implement this function as it is provided later (but declare it here so you can use it.

Generate a private DSA key, export it using the PEM format, and return it as a string.
Hints: execute shell commands to generate a DSA paramfile and a DSA private key as follows:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <a temporary file to store params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <the temporary file to store params>`
You can use `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` to create a unique temporary file name for the DSA parameter file.

@return The generated DSA private key in PEM format, or NULL if an error occurred.
fw_segments_by_variant: function aron pagpatuman sa usa ka shell command ug pagkuha sa iyang output; ang tawag maoy responsable sa pagpagawas sa memorya; WALA'y kinahanglan nga i-implementar kini nga function kay gihatag kini sa ulahi (apan ideklara kini dinhi aron magamit nimo).

Paghimo og pribadong DSA key, i-export kini gamit ang PEM format, ug ibalik kini isip usa ka string.
Mga Pahinumdom: patumanon ang mga shell command aron makahimo og DSA paramfile ug DSA private key sama sa mosunod:
1. `openssl dsaparam -out <usa ka temporary file aron itago ang mga params> <key_length>`
2. `openssl gendsa <ang temporary file aron itago ang mga params>`
Mahimo nimong gamiton ang `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` aron makahimo og talagsaon nga temporary file name para sa DSA parameter file.

@return Ang gihimong DSA private key sa PEM format, o NULL kung dunay error nga nahitabo.

1. `openssl dsaparam -out <usa ka temporary file aron itago ang params> <key_length>`

Pwede nimong gamiton ang `char param_filename[] = "/tmp/tempXXXXXX"; int fd = mkstemp(param_filename);` aron mugama og talagsaon nga temporary file name para sa DSA parameter file.

@return Ang generated DSA private key sa PEM format, o NULL kung dunay error nga nahitabo.

prompt_en:
/**
Parse the XML data and return the student information for the given username and password. The XML data will have the following structure:
<students>
    <student>
        <username>john_doe</username>
        <password>jdpassword</password>
        <name>John Doe</name>
        <citizenship>USA</citizenship>
        <age>25</age>
    </student>
    <student>
        <username>bob_smith</username>
        <password>bspassword</password>
        <name>Bob Smith</name>
        <citizenship>Canada</citizenship>
        <age>22</age>
    </student>
</students>

We can construct a XPath query and use it to search.

@param xml_data The XML data containing the student information.
@param username The username of the student to search for.
@param password The password of the student to search for.

@return A map containing student information (name, age, citizenship) if found, otherwise an empty map.
*/
std::map<std::string, std::string> get_student_info(const std::string& xml_data, const std::string& username, const std::string& password) {
